channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US warns of military action against vessels laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: On Friday, April 24, 2026, US President Donald Trump announced that he had instructed military forces to 'shoot and kill' Iranian boats involved in laying mines in the Strait of Hormuz, a critical mar...

item.title: Three family members killed, three wounded in Zamboanga police operation
item.description: In Zamboanga City, a police operation targeting a suspected bandit ended tragically when three members of a local family were killed and three others were wounded. The fatalities were identified as Je...

item.title: Private sector calls for key reforms to help Tanzania reach $1 trillion economy by 2050
item.description: Business leaders in Tanzania have outlined several important reforms needed to support the country’s ambition of becoming a $1 trillion economy by 2050. They emphasised that the private sector will pl...

item.title: Western Bulldogs suffer further injury setbacks in heavy loss to Sydney Swans
item.description: The Western Bulldogs endured another tough night as they were thrashed by 11 goals against the Sydney Swans at Marvel Stadium, extending their recent poor form. While star forward Aaron Naughton avoid...

item.title: Cape Town responds to flooding, N1 crash causes closure, and electricity market reform progresses
item.description: Cape Town authorities have been working around the clock to respond to widespread flooding following heavy weekend rains. The City’s Urban Mobility Directorate, together with Disaster Risk Management ...

item.title: How artificial intelligence is used by online casinos to analyse player behaviour and improve services
item.description: Artificial intelligence (AI) is increasingly being used in online gambling to help casinos better understand player behaviour by analysing large amounts of digital data in real time. Instead of relyin...

item.title: Decline in Innu child protection removals, but Investigations Still on the Rise
item.description: Innu children remain overrepresented in the child protection system, but the rate of their removal from homes has dropped significantly over the past several years. According to a recent report presen...

item.title: Seth Rollins Walks Off NFL Network Set During Draft Coverage After Teasing
item.description: WWE star Seth Rollins had a tense moment during a guest appearance on 'Good Morning Football' ahead of the NFL Draft. The former WWE Champion had experienced a difficult week, losing his WrestleMania ...

item.title: Paranormal YouTuber and UFO writer found dead by suicide after mental health crisis
item.description: A well-known paranormal YouTuber and UFO writer tragically died by suicide in his home following a mental health crisis. Authorities were alerted when a call was made to the police regarding his menta...

item.title: FCT Minister Waives C of O Fees for Nigerian Law School, Orders Expansion of Staff and Student Facilities
item.description: The Minister of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT), Nyesom Wike, has approved a waiver of fees for the Certificate of Occupancy (C of O) of the Nigerian Law School campus located in Bwari Area Counci...

item.title: Analysis of African Teams' Participation in the 2026 World Cup and Its Influence on Sports Betting
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup will see a historic inclusion of 10 African countries, increasing excitement both on the pitch and in the sports betting sector across the continent. With the tournament hosted...

item.title: Cagayan de Oro Housewife Claims P10 Million Lotto Jackpot
item.description: A 50-year-old housewife from Cagayan de Oro City has finally won a major lottery prize after over a decade of placing bets. On April 7, she purchased a P25 Lucky Pick ticket for the Lotto 6/42 draw of...

item.title: Assessing Europe’s Economic and Geopolitical Challenges in Relation to Canada
item.description: The article argues that Canada should reconsider its idealization of Europe as a model for culture, economics, and governance. While some Canadians admire European public culture, bilingualism, and we...

item.title: Utah Startup Develops Lab-Grown Human Sperm to Aid Male Infertility
item.description: Paterna Biosciences, a biotech startup based in Utah, claims to have successfully produced functional human sperm in a laboratory setting and used it to fertilize eggs, creating visually healthy embry...

item.title: Police confirm no rape occurred outside Epsom church after investigation
item.description: Surrey Police have concluded their investigation into a reported rape outside a church in Epsom earlier this month, confirming that no sexual offence took place. The police investigation followed publ...

item.title: Sara Cox appointed new host of BBC Radio 2 breakfast show following Scott Mills departure
item.description: BBC DJ Sara Cox has been appointed as the new host of Radio 2's breakfast show, replacing Scott Mills. Cox, originally from Bolton, has been presenting Radio 2's teatime show since 2019 and expressed ...

item.title: Kimani Ichung’wah reveals why he blocked Uhuru Kenyatta after a heated argument
item.description: Kikuyu MP Kimani Ichung’wah recently made shocking revelations during an interview with Oga Obinna. The politician disclosed that he was introduced to President William Ruto by former President Uhuru ...

item.title: China Announces Expansion of Island Facilities and Plans for Nuclear Aircraft Carrier
item.description: China has released a video suggesting the development of its fourth aircraft carrier, potentially nuclear-powered, alongside plans to continue expanding its claimed islands. The video, titled 'Into th...

item.title: Olly Robbins Prevented Cabinet Office from Accessing Mandelson's Vetting Summary
item.description: Cat Little, a senior civil servant in the Cabinet Office, has revealed that Olly Robbins, the former head of the Foreign Office, refused to provide a summary of Peter Mandelson's security vetting to h...

item.title: Toronto Subway Musicians Enliven Daily Commutes with Live Performances
item.description: Toronto's subway stations are home to a vibrant community of musicians who perform regularly for commuters. Among them is cellist Leo Zhang, who has played underground for nearly 30 years, offering pe...
